steps.title: Find cos(A) and determine angle A
steps.content: In right triangle ABC with right angle at C, use the cosine ratio with AC = 19 (adjacent to angle A) and AB = 38 (hypotenuse): $$\cos(A) = \frac{19}{38} = \frac{1}{2}$$ This is the cosine of a special angle: $\cos(60°) = \frac{1}{2}$, so $A = 60°$.
